description: This scatter plot presents exhaustive screening results illustrating the relationship between feature number and cross-validation scores for machine learning prediction of Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si steel microhardness. The x-axis shows the number of features ranging from 1 to 5, while the y-axis displays cross-validation scores spanning approximately 0.2 to 1.4. With only 1 feature, scores exhibit high variability (0.3–1.3), indicating unstable single-feature model performance. As features increase to 2, the distribution narrows to roughly 0.25–0.7. Critically, at 3 features, scores concentrate in a lower range (approximately 0.25–0.35), demonstrating optimal model stability. This aligns with the study's identification of three key parameters—shear modulus (G), difference in cohesive energy (δ_Ec), and local cohesive energy mismatch (D_Ec)—from modified solid solution strengthening theory. The trend confirms that 3 features constitute the optimal configuration for the support vector regression model, as adding more features (4–5) yields no performance improvement, validating the feature selection strategy for high-accuracy steel property prediction.
